rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for bindings after subcomponents and connections.
Use visible feature names and legal constants in AGREE contracts; avoid subcomponent ports in type-level contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes to maintain architectural integrity.
Align helper declaration types with expression types to prevent type mismatch errors.
Use named constants and visible relations in AGREE contracts for clarity and traceability.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must use valid AADL scope syntax and visible identifiers.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should use proper type alignment (e.g., bool = int >= int).
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts.
